originalTitle: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
title: Super News for Cameroon! President Paul Gets a Big Helper!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! Your super kid-friendly news reporter is here with some exciting news from the beautiful country of Cameroon! Cameroon is a country right here in Africa, just like where you live. It's known for its amazing mountains, lovely beaches, and kind people.

Meet President Paul Biya

Every country has a leader, just like your family has a leader (maybe a mom, dad, or grandma!). For Cameroon, the big leader is President Paul Biya. He is a very, very experienced man. Imagine someone who has been helping to lead the country for a super long time! He has been President for 43 years! Wow, that's longer than your parents have been alive, and even longer than many of your grandparents! President Biya is 93 years old. That's a lot of birthdays! He has seen so many things and helped his country for many, many years. Being a President is a very important job. It's like being the head teacher for the whole country, or the captain of a very big team. The President works hard to make sure everyone in the country is safe, happy, and has what they need. Did you know? Cameroon is sometimes called "Africa in miniature" because it has so many different kinds of beautiful places, just like a little version of our whole big continent!

A New Helper for President Paul!

Now, here is the super exciting news! After all these years, President Paul Biya is getting a big helper! This helper is called a 'deputy'. Can you say 'deputy'? It means someone who helps the main person in charge. Think of it like this: if you're building a tall tower with blocks, and your friend helps you by handing you the blocks, your friend is your helper, or your deputy! Having a deputy is a fantastic idea because it means the President will have someone special to share all the important work with. Imagine if you had to do all your homework, all your chores, and organize all your games all by yourself! It would be a lot of work, right? But if a friend or sibling helps you, it makes everything much easier and more fun!

Why is a Helper So Good?

Sharing is caring, and sharing work makes everything lighter! The President has many jobs. He has to think about schools for children like you, roads for cars and buses, hospitals for when people get sick, and making sure everyone has enough food and clean water. That's a very big list! With a deputy, the President can share some of these jobs. The deputy can visit different parts of Cameroon, listen to what people need, and bring those ideas back to the President. It's like having two sets of eyes and two brains working together for the good of the country. This means more things can get done, and perhaps even faster! Did you know? Teamwork makes the dream work! When people work together, they can achieve amazing things, just like ants working together to carry a big leaf!

What Does a President Do Anyway?

Let's talk a little more about what Presidents do. They are like the super-planners for the country. They make decisions about big things. For example, they decide if a new school should be built in your town, or if there should be more parks for children to play in. They also meet with leaders from other countries. It's like having a playdate with friends from another village, but on a much bigger scale! These meetings help Cameroon work with other countries to solve big problems, like making sure everyone has access to good medicines or sharing ideas about protecting our beautiful planet.

A Very Long Time as President

President Paul Biya has been President for 43 years. That's a super long time! Can you imagine doing the same important job for 43 years? He has seen many children grow up, go to school, and become adults. He has guided Cameroon through many changes and helped it grow. Now, having a deputy means that this very experienced leader will have another smart person right by his side. It's like having a co-captain on a football team. The main captain is still in charge, but the co-captain helps lead and inspire the team too. Did you know? Leaders of countries are usually chosen by the people! Everyone who is old enough gets to vote for who they think will be the best leader.

Good News for Kids in Cameroon!

So, what does this mean for you, my young friends in Cameroon? When the leaders of your country work well together, it helps everyone. It means they can make even better plans for new schools, better hospitals, and more fun places for you to visit. It's a step towards making Cameroon even stronger and better for all its people, especially for the children who are the future. Having a deputy helps make sure that the country's work is always taken care of, even when the President is super busy. It's a wonderful example of how asking for help and working with others can make a big difference! So, let's cheer for President Paul Biya and his new deputy! We hope they work together wonderfully to make Cameroon shine even brighter. That's all the news from your kid-friendly reporter for now. Stay curious and keep learning!
summary: Cameroon's very experienced President Paul Biya, who is 93 years old and has led the country for 43 years, is getting a special helper called a deputy for the first time to share the important work of leading the country.
whyMatters: This is important because having a helper means the President can share big jobs, making it easier to take care of everyone in Cameroon, like building schools and making sure people are safe and happy.

originalTitle: Liverpool's FA Cup capitulation ramps up pressure on Slot
title: Liverpool's FA Cup capitulation ramps up pressure on Slot
content: Nobody would have believed that 12 months on from Liverpool's title win, a fanbase so united behind its team and its manager would be, at best, apathetic as to whether Arne Slot remains in the job.

title: Haaland's 'old-school' lesson for Liverpool as FA Cup record falls
content: Manchester City have beaten Arsenal and taken apart Liverpool in their past two games and Erling Haaland's return to goalscoring form has laid down the gauntlet for the run-in.

title: Rohl won't watch title rivals as Rangers enjoy top spot
content: As Rangers move to the top of the Scottish Premiership for the first time this season, head coach Danny Rohl is "convinced" they can finish the job.
